pois.title: Toyokawa Cherry Blossom Grove

pois.title: Toyokawa Cherry Blossom Grove
pois.description: This grove of cherry trees in Memorial Park serves as a living symbol of friendship between Cupertino and Toyokawa, Aichi, Japan. In 1983, 200 cherry trees arrived in Cupertino from Toyokawa. Today, these trees are the star of the annual Cupertino Cherry Blossom Festival which reminds us of their historical significance while celebrating their springtime bloom.

pois.title: Sister Cities Bench
pois.description: Beautifully decorated bench next to the bus stop celebrates the Cupertino-Toyokawa sister city relationship since 1976. pois.title: In Memory Of John Minnick
pois.description: This memorial bench honors Professor John Minnick, a dedicated Mathematics educator at De Anza College who made a lasting impact on generations of students. The bench serves as a place of reflection and remembrance, celebrating his contributions to the academic community and his passion for teaching.

pois.title: De Anza College Farmers' Market
pois.description: A lively open-air market where local farmers, artisans, and food vendors gather each week to offer fresh produce, handmade goods, and local specialties. The De Anza College Farmers' Market celebrates sustainability, healthy living, and community connection, making it a favorite weekend destination for residents and visitors alike.
pois.address: 10000 South Stelling Road, Cupertino, CA, USA, Cupertino, US

pois.title: De Anza Flea Market
pois.description: A bustling community marketplace in Cupertino where local vendors and residents gather to buy, sell, and trade unique items, handmade crafts, antiques, and collectibles. The De Anza Flea Market is a vibrant reflection of the city’s diverse culture and entrepreneurial spirit, offering a fun way for visitors to explore local creativity and connect with others in the community.
